'''Coruscul''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]] situated in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It was the site of a hidden [[Fortress/Legends|stronghold]] at one point used by the [[Order of the Silver Jedi]] sect of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. After he was liberated from a dead-in-[[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Outrider-class cruiser|starship]] at some point during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] existence, the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]–affiliated being known as the [[Force vampire]] began travelling to Coruscul in order to destroy the stronghold there.

==Description==
Coruscul was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Polyhedron" /> located in the [[Coruscul system/Legends|Coruscul system]], which was a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:Jedi_symbol.svg|thumb|right|300px|Coruscul was the site of a stronghold of the Order of the Silver Jedi, a sect of the Jedi Order (emblem depicted).]]
During the reign of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Coruscul was the site of a [[Fortress/Legends|stronghold]] of the [[Order of the Silver Jedi]], a sect of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="Polyhedron" /> At some point between [[86 BBY/Legends|86 BBY]] and [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],<ref name="Dates">{{PolyhedronCite|104|Dangers from the Dark Side: Villains for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign, Part Two}} establishes that [[Force vampire|Tel Angor]], who was ninety [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's existence]], was transformed into the [[The Force/Legends|Force]] vampire no later than several years before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|beginning]] of the Clone Wars to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and the [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|establishment]] of the New Republic to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], therefore placing Angor's transformation sometime between [[86 BBY/Legends|86 BBY]] and [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]].</ref> an unsuccessful experiment by the former Order resulted in the transformation of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Force vampire|Tel Angor]] into the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]–affiliated being known as the [[The Force/Legends|Force]] vampire. Although the Force vampire proceeded to destroy any records he could find concerning his creation, he never visited the Coruscul stronghold—the last location where such data was stored—before he became trapped aboard an [[Outrider-class cruiser|''Outrider''-class cruiser]] that remained dead in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] until after the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|established]]<ref name="Polyhedron" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Dates" /> After a New Republic [[Picket ship/Legends|picket ship]] unwillingly liberated the Force vampire, he began travelling to Coruscul aboard the [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger starliner]] ''[[Dantooine's Pride]]'' with the goal of destroying the stronghold that both served as a last reminder of the Order of the Silver Jedi and contained evidence of the vampire's existence.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Locations==
Coruscul was the site of a hidden stronghold that at one point was used by the Order of the Silver Jedi. Records documenting the creation and [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] of the Force vampire were entered into [[Computer/Legends|computer]] logs at the Coruscul stronghold, and a [[Unidentified data journal|data journal]] similarly describing the entity's genesis was also kept there.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
Coruscul was mentioned in "[[Dangers from the Dark Side: Villains for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign, Part Two]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Michele Carter]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 104|104th issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[February]] [[1995]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Coruscul [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Coruscul itself, in grid square L-10.<ref name="TEA" />
